key_insight: Post-training methods change when confidence is trustworthy during chain-of-thought reasoning: OPD is best before reasoning, SFT is best for early stopping, and RL is best for aggregating final answers.
engineering_relevance: A stage-aware, position-aware confidence policy can improve reasoning systems' accuracy and token efficiency by using confidence only where it is calibrated.
method: Three-stage calibration: pre-reasoning, during reasoning, and post-reasoning confidence analysis.
Compare SFT, RL, and OPD on mathematical reasoning benchmarks for difficulty estimation, early termination, and answer aggregation.
PosConf uses confidence only in reliable relative-position intervals to avoid miscalibrated regions.
evidence_signal: On controlled math reasoning benchmarks, PosConf improved RL answer aggregation by 6.1 points over majority voting and improved OPD early stopping by up to 4.3 points under tight token budgets.
watch_item: Confidence reliability shifts with position, so a fixed threshold or single-stage calibration policy may fail when the model enters inverse-calibrated regions.
paper_title: Post-Training Shifts Confidence: A Three-Stage Analysis of How SFT, RL, and OPD Shape Pre-, Intra-, and Post-CoT Calibration
